tree.name: CJ McCollum
tree.narrative: **CJ McCollum** arrived in the trade that ended the **Trae Young** era in Atlanta. The Hawks sent their franchise point guard to Washington in January 2026, getting back McCollum and **Corey Kispert**. It's a full-circle moment: Young only became a Hawk because Atlanta drafted **Luka Dončić** third overall in 2018 and swapped him to Dallas for the #5 pick.
tree.tradeDescription: Hawks acquire CJ McCollum and Corey Kispert for Trae Young

assetsGivenUp.name: Trae Young
assetsGivenUp.narrative: **Trae Young** was the Hawks' franchise cornerstone from 2018 to 2026 — a 3x All-Star who led Atlanta to the 2021 ECF. He came to Atlanta in the famous draft-night swap where the Hawks picked **Luka Dončić** #3 and traded him to Dallas for the #5 pick. After seven seasons, Young was dealt to Washington for McCollum and Kispert.
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Draft night swap: Hawks selected Luka Doncic #3, traded him to Dallas for the #5 pick (Trae Young) and a 2019 protected 1st rounder
assetsGivenUp.note: 3x All-Star, Hawks franchise cornerstone 2018-2026. The Luka-Trae swap defined both franchises.

assetsGivenUp.name: 2018 1st Round Pick (#3)
assetsGivenUp.narrative: Atlanta's own #3 overall pick in 2018, used to select **Luka Dončić** and immediately traded to Dallas in the swap that brought **Trae Young** to Atlanta.
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Hawks selected Doncic #3 overall, immediately traded to Dallas in swap for #5 pick (Trae Young)
assetsGivenUp.note: Slovenian prodigy, EuroLeague MVP at 19. Became 5x All-Star with Mavericks.
